com.nexacro.java.xapi.data

Class Variable

  • java.lang.Object
    • com.nexacro.java.xapi.data.Variable
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    java.io.Serializable, java.lang.Cloneable

    public class Variable
extends java.lang.Object
implements java.lang.Cloneable, java.io.Serializable

    indicate variable saving data, and consist of Identifier(name) and value. Value is saved after transformed according to data type. For data type, refer to DataTypes.

    if data type is not designated, it is set by discriminating data type on the moment where inirially setting data. For example, case as below, DataTypes.INT is set for var data type. 

     Variable var = new Variable("age");
 var.set(20);

 assertEquals(DataTypes.INT, var.getType());

    the caveat is, when setting value by summoning as set(Object), if identical data value to the data type is set, data modification could occur.

    Author:
    Choi, Jongkwan
    See Also:
    Serialized Form

      • isConvertingToDataType

        public boolean isConvertingToDataType()
        Inspect transformation status to data type when data setting. Basic value is "true". i.e. if "true", value in transformed to data type when setting, and if "false", it is saved without data modification.

        for example, if Variable data type is int type, and set("123") is summoned, data is as below.

        isConvertingToDataType value Setted data
        true Integer with 123 value
        false String with "123" value
        Returns:
        transformation status to data type
        See Also:
        setConvertingToDataType(boolean)

      • setConvertingToDataType

        public void setConvertingToDataType(boolean isConvertingToDataType)
        Set transdormation status to data type when setting data.
        Parameters:
        isConvertingToDataType - transformation status to data type
        See Also:
        isConvertingToDataType

        public VariableDataConverter getDataConverter()

      • getCurrentDataConverter

        public VariableDataConverter getCurrentDataConverter()
        Return current VariableDataConverter. If separate VariableDataConverter is unset, basically DefaultVariableDataConverteris applied.
        Returns:
        VariableDataConverter
        See Also:
        getDataConverter(), setDataConverter(VariableDataConverter), DefaultVariableDataConverter
